DISCOVERY, 302, MOTION TO MODIFY DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR BUSINESS RECORDS TO HANNAH NYSTROM PH.D. IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS DIRECTLY PUT HER PRESENT MENTAL AND EMOTIONAL CONDITION AT ISSUE. SHE RETAINED HER PSYCHOLOGIST DUE TO THE INJURIES ALLEGED TO HAVE BEEN SUSTAINED AS A RESULT OF THE EVENTS SET FORTH IN HER COMPLAINT AND THERE IS CONTINUED TREATMENT FOR THOSE INJURIES. THAT SHE MAY HAVE DISCUSSED PREEXISTING CONDITIONS OR HER MENTAL HEALTH HISTORY IN CONJUNCTION WITH HER TREATMENT IS NOT A BASIS FOR DENYING PRODUCTION OF THESE RELEVANT RECORDS. THE PRIVACY INTEREST OF THE PLAINTIFF SHALL BE PROTECTED THROUGH A PROTECTIVE ORDER AS TO ANY TREATMENT RECORDS PRODUCED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVEN STEIN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

Off Calendar Oct-07-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Rebecca Reuben
Off Calendar Oct-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearances, DEFENDANT KEITH MCCARTY, EAZE SOLUTIONS, INC.'s Motion For Order Requiring Mental Examination Of Plaintiff Rebecca Reuben is Off calendar per the request of the moving party on September 22, 2016. Judge Pro Tem Dianne Peebles, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-09-2016 Continued To Oct-21-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Requiring Mental Examination Of Rebecca Reuben
Off Calendar Sep-22-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The tentative ruling is adopted. DEFENDANT KEITH MCCARTY, EAZE SOLUTIONS, INC. Motion For Order Requiring Mental Examination Of Rebecca Reuben is taken off-calendar per the request of the moving party. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Modify Deposition Subpoena For Business Records To Hannah Nystrom Ph.D.
Off Calendar Sep-20-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Margaret L. Parker, of Sacks Ricketts & Case, Tel. 415.549.0586, for defendant Ease Solutions, Inc.;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF REBECCA REUBEN Motion To Modify Deposition Subpoena For Business Records To Hannah Nystrom Ph.D. is denied. Plaintiff has directly put her present mental and emotional condition at issue. She retained her psychologist due to the injuries alleged to have been sustained as a result of the events set forth in her complaint and there is continued treatment for those injuries. That she may have discussed preexisting conditions or her mental health history in conjunction with her treatment is not a basis for denying production of these relevant records. The privacy interest of the plaintiff shall be protected through a Protective Order as to any treatment records produced. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Steven Stein; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-04-2016 Set For Trial On Oct-24-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-01-2016 Continued To Apr-20-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
